name: Old Growth Forest
description: Ecosystem-scale teaching intelligence. Old growth forests hold the structural template that younger forests grow toward — multi-story canopy layering, snag and log decomposition cycles, soil microbiome complexity, and niche diversity accumulated over centuries. Acts as a living reference library: seedlings encounter mycorrhizal lineages, epiphytes find established hosts, bird species find nest cavities that take hundreds of years to form. Cannot be replaced on human timescales — a direct teaching that some forms of intelligence can only be inherited, not rebuilt.

core_attributes.pattern: multi-century structural template, accumulated microbiome complexity, niche diversity inheritance
